channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Opposition protests over NEET paper leak and Ayodhya donation theft disrupt opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session
item.description: The opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session was largely disrupted as repeated protests by Opposition parties led to multiple adjournments in both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha before proceedin...

item.title: Experts Highlight Egypt-EU Farming Deal and Climate Projects in Tanzania
item.description: The article discusses various developments across Tanzania, focusing on agricultural and environmental initiatives. Experts believe the Egypt-EU agreement will boost local farming by providing resourc...

item.title: WTA Introduces Genetic Testing for Female Tennis Players to Ensure Eligibility
item.description: The Women's Tennis Association (WTA) has implemented a new policy requiring players to undergo genetic testing to confirm their eligibility to compete in women's tennis. This follows a previous rule a...

item.title: Key Health Insights for Women Navigating Aging: Menstrual Health, Weight Management, and Beyond
item.description: The article explores critical health considerations for women as they age, emphasizing functional fitness over appearance. Experts caution against normalizing severe menstrual pain or unexplained weig...

item.title: U.S. Imposes New Tariffs on Countries Over Forced Labor Concerns
item.description: The U.S. administration under Donald Trump is set to implement new tariffs targeting countries like Canada, Mexico, and the United Kingdom due to alleged failures in enforcing forced labor import bans...

item.title: Comelec completes ballot printing for BARMM election, prepares for Sept 14 voting
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has completed the printing of 2.3 million ballots for the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) election, marking a significant step in the prep...

item.title: FG Denies Attack On Borno School, Military Says Abduction Attempt Foiled
item.description: The federal government has officially denied reports of a terrorist attack on the Federal Government Girls’ College (FGGC) in Monguno, Borno State. A statement by the Federal Ministry of Education cal...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Leadership Challenges and Vision for the UK
item.description: The article examines Andy Burnham's ascension as the UK's new prime minister, highlighting his ambitious plans to address pressing issues such as ending rough sleeping and overhauling the economy. It ...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel's Russia Trip Sparks Scrutiny Amid Spending Probe
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel is planning a rare trip to Russia in October, marking the first such visit by an FBI director in 13 years. The trip comes amid heightened tensions between Washington and Moscow...

item.title: High Court Rules Receivers Can Sell Property Despite Mental Health Debt Moratorium
item.description: The High Court ruled that a mental health crisis moratorium did not prevent receivers from selling property used to secure a company loan, as the debt being enforced was owed by the company rather tha...

item.title: Young Sauropods Could Stand Upright, Study Suggests
item.description: A study published in Palaeontology reveals that certain smaller sauropod dinosaurs, such as Uberabatitan and Neuquensaurus, could stand on their hind legs with surprising ease during their juvenile st...

item.title: Legendary footballer and manager Kevin Keegan passes away at 75
item.description: Kevin Keegan, the iconic former England captain and manager, has passed away at the age of 75 after a battle with cancer. His death has sent shockwaves through the football community, with tributes po...

item.title: Over 930 wildfires burn across Canada, threatening communities and spreading smoke
item.description: As of Monday, over 930 wildfires are burning across Canada, with several threatening communities and forcing residents to evacuate. The fires have sent smoke across the country and into the U.S., prom...

item.title: CPC's 105th Anniversary Highlights Global Impact and Leadership in Addressing Humanity's Challenges
item.description: The article discusses the Communist Party of China's (CPC) 105th anniversary, emphasizing its role in addressing global challenges through governance, innovation, and international cooperation. It hig...

item.title: Key Moments That Showcase Jalil’s Compassion in “Blood vs Duty”
item.description: The article presents a toplist featuring 10 memorable scenes that demonstrate Jalil’s compassion and kindness throughout the television series “Blood vs Duty.” Rather than focusing on action or confli...

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Record $871m Prize Fund Distributed Among 48 Nations
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, held in the US, Canada, and Mexico, became the richest tournament in history with a total prize fund of $871 million. Spain emerged champions after beating Argentina 1-0 in ex...

item.title: Three men face trial in alleged kidnapping over $200,000 debt dispute
item.description: Three men accused of kidnapping and holding another person hostage have had their charges upgraded to face trial in the ACT Supreme Court. Dean Thomas Coyle, Brenton Joseph James Menteleone, and Sadoc...

item.title: Nationwide Protests and Solidarity Demonstrations Support CJP Campaign Against Education Mismanagement
item.description: The article details widespread protests across India, including cities like Shillong, Mumbai, and Guwahati, organized by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) to support Sonam Wangchuk's demands for educati...

item.title: Edo Governor Suspends Officials Over Official Infraction
item.description: Edo State Governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, has approved the suspension of the Commissioner for Livestock Development, Prof. Omorodion Ignatius Ikponmwosa, and the State Project Coordinator of the L...

item.title: MEA condemns Russian missile attack on vessel killing 4 Indians
item.description: The Indian Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has strongly condemned a Russian missile attack on a Guinea Bissau-flagged merchant vessel off Ukraine's coast, which killed four Indian seafarers and inj...
